rag_rules: Fix type mismatches in AGREE expressions by ensuring left and right sides of '<=' have matching types, such as int or real.
Resolve '<error>' type diagnostics by tracing unresolved identifiers or invalid helper declarations on the same line and correcting or removing the clause.
Avoid placing implementation-level references or subcomponent paths in component type annexes; move valid bindings to implementation scope or remove invalid guarantees.
Do not use Boolean data ports in numeric range comparisons unless the requirement and AADL data representation support scalar numeric values.
generic_rules: In AGREE contracts, ensure all expressions use valid, visible identifiers and declared helpers from the same scope.
When generating AGREE guarantees, validate that types match between operands in binary expressions like '<=' or comparisons.
Use only visible AADL features or declared auxiliary variables in implementation-level assignments and guarantees.
If a guarantee references an invalid or unresolved identifier, remove the clause rather than introducing new architecture.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE expressions must have consistent types; e.g., int comparisons should not mix with <error> or real types.
AGREE constants and equations must be declared with valid scalar types: int, real, or bool.
Implementation-level guarantees should reference valid output features or declared variables, not subcomponent paths in type scopes.
Do not assume that Boolean ports can be used in numeric range checks without explicit scalar representation.
anti_patterns: Do not attempt to fix type errors by adding new architecture; instead, trace and correct the root cause of unresolved identifiers.
Avoid placing implementation-level bindings in component type annexes unless they reference valid outputs or declared variables.
Do not attempt to convert opaque Boolean or enumeration data into numeric comparisons without clear scalar semantics.
Do not introduce package-qualified identifiers in AGREE code that are not present in the input AADL model.
irrelevant_topics: AADL package references and with clauses
AGREE annex placement in component types vs implementations
AGREE assume/guarantee syntax and validator diagnostics
Model fusion and scope preservation rules
